title: Don't use allowedValues for a location parameter
description: Don't use "allowedValues" for a location parameterAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalreliabilitymaintainabilityCode SmellIn Azure Resource Manager (ARM) templates, it is possible to setallowedValuesfor various parameters. This field is used to define a list of valid values for a parameter. This is a way to restrict the input values provided when deploying or updating a resource using the template.However, when it comes to a parameter defining thelocationof a resource, this practice can lead to a code smell.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Don't use allowedValues for location parameters. This limits deployment flexibility across Azure regions.
